terms.definition: E[X], the probability-weighted average of a random variable; also called the mean μ of X.
Var(X) = E[(X − E[X])²] = E[X²] − (E[X])²; measures squared spread around the mean.
σ = √Var(X); spread in the same units as X, unlike variance.
E[aX + bY + c] = aE[X] + bE[Y] + c for constants a, b, c; holds without independence.
x̄ = (1/n) Σ xᵢ; an estimator of E[X] from observed data.
E[g(X)] computed as Σ g(x)p(x) or ∫ g(x)f(x)dx without first deriving the distribution of g(X).
